title: melica ciliata subsp. glauca
intro: Melica ciliata subsp. glauca, also known as blue melic grass, is a subspecies of the Melica ciliata plant, a type of grass that belongs to the Poaceae family. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that help to soothe and heal wounds, reduce fever, and alleviate digestive issues. The health benefits of blue melic grass include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve digestive health, and boost the immune system, making it a potential remedy for various health conditions. Herbal preparations of blue melic grass can be made into teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed to reap its medicinal benefits.
